artifacts.content:
Hi Ms. Patel,

I hope your week is going okay. I wanted to touch base about Ethan and a couple of practical things as we head into the winter term.

First, thank you again for being so mindful about his asthma and allergy. As a reminder, his asthma is mild and pretty well-controlled, mostly exercise- and cold-induced. He uses his blue Ventolin (salbutamol) inhaler before vigorous activity or if he starts to wheeze. His last big flare that needed an ER visit was back in March 2022 after a very intense indoor soccer game – nothing that serious since, and he’s never had to stay overnight for asthma.

The tree nut allergy is still the bigger day-to-day worry. He’s especially reactive to walnuts and pecans. We keep an EpiPen Jr. with him at all times. Please just continue what you’ve been doing: no food sharing, and he should avoid any baked goods or snacks that don’t clearly say nut‑free. If he ever shows hives, swelling around his lips or eyes, trouble breathing, or vomiting, please use the EpiPen and call 911 right away, then call me or his dad.

He also wears glasses for nearsightedness, and he has a habit of taking them off when he’s tired or when he’s drawing. If he says he can’t see the board or screen clearly, it almost always means the glasses are off or smudged somewhere.

On the emotional side, there have been some family changes since last year. His dad, Kevin, and I separated amicably in late 2023. Ethan lives primarily with me at Willowbank Crescent and goes to Kevin’s place in Ajax every other weekend and one weeknight. Mondays after those weekends can be a bit tender – sometimes he’s quiet, clingy, or more tearful at pickup. If you notice him becoming withdrawn or unusually frustrated, a quick check‑in or letting him draw for a few minutes usually helps him reset. He does really well with calm, clear instructions and a bit of advance notice before transitions (e.g., “In five minutes we’re going to tidy up and line up for gym”).

Thank you again for everything you do for him. Please feel free to email or call me at (416) 555‑2398 if anything comes up.

Best,
Laura Michelle Thompson
laura.thompson83@example.com

metadata.subject: Ethan – health and transition notes for winter term

artifacts.content:
Hi Mom,

Just wanted to catch you up before the week gets away from me.

I’ve been seeing that therapist I told you about – her name is Dr. Hannah Kline, she’s in the Women’s Mental Health Program at Sunnybrook, E Wing, 3rd floor. I go every other Thursday over my lunch break. It’s been surprisingly helpful. We spent a whole session talking about that panic-y episode I had after one of Ethan’s weekends away, the one I messaged her about in MyChart under “Follow-up re: panic episode after Ethan’s weekend away.” It still feels silly to write that out, but she keeps reminding me that anxiety doesn’t care how “together” you look on the outside.

We also went back over that scare in October 2023 when I ended up in the Sunnybrook Emergency Department in T Wing with the chest pain and dizziness. Remember how they ran all the tests because they were worried about my heart, and then the discharge summary said “Non-cardiac chest pain – likely anxiety”? I’ve been carrying that around like a secret shame. Dr. Kline thinks that was my body’s way of saying, “Enough,” with the separation, work, and everything.

Speaking of the separation, I’ve been trying really hard not to dwell on that last mediation session on Yonge Street in November 2023, but it still pops up. When Kevin admitted to that “brief emotional affair” with the woman from his firm, it was like a switch flipped. That was the night I wrote that journal entry I titled “Why I can’t go back after today.” I don’t regret the decision, but some days I still feel like I failed Ethan by not keeping the family together.

On a more mundane note: can you still do pickup next Monday if there’s freezing rain? Daniel “the family Uber” to the rescue? I’ll send you home with another safe granola bar stash and double-check there’s a spare Ventolin and EpiPen Jr. in your front hall drawer.

Love you,
Laura

metadata.subject: Quick update (therapy, that scare, and next week)

artifacts.content:
Title: Monday brain dump – work / money / Ethan

Work
- Got that confidential email from Elena Ruiz back in June 2024 (“Upcoming restructuring – please keep confidential”) about the research + outpatient clinic department maybe merging with another unit in early 2025. Still no concrete update, but it’s hanging over me.
- If they reclassify my Senior Administrative Coordinator role and move us fully to the Bayview campus full‑time, I’ll lose the hybrid schedule. No more two days from the condo on Willowbank.
- I keep spiraling: if I’m on-site 8:30–4:30 every day, how do I juggle Ethan’s asthma and allergy appointments, PD days, and Monday pickups? I don’t want to be “that mom” at work, but I also can’t be casual about his Ventolin/EpiPen routine.

Money
- Since Jan 2024 I’ve been carrying around $7,500 on the Scotiabank Visa – mostly legal fees for the separation and out‑of‑pocket stuff for Ethan’s allergist and optometrist at the Toronto East Medical Centre.
- Spreadsheet on laptop: “Post-Separation Finances 2024.xlsx.” Need to update it with last month’s grocery creep and the new soccer registration fee.
- Feel guilty even thinking about a yoga workshop when I’m paying interest on that card.

Ethan / Kevin
- Monday mornings after Ajax weekends are still rough. He’s woken up teary a few times since Feb 2024 saying, “Dad and Jenna were arguing again last night.”
- Draft email to Dr. Kline sitting in MyChart titled “Worried about what Ethan is seeing at Kevin’s place.” Haven’t hit send.
- I don’t want to overreact or turn this into ammunition in case the separation agreement from Dec 2023 (Ontario Court of Justice, 311 Jarvis, “Thompson-Separation-Order-Dec2023.pdf”) ever gets dragged back into court, but I also can’t ignore it if he’s seeing shouting matches.

Action items
- Email Elena re: any timeline on restructuring.
- Update budget spreadsheet this weekend.
- Talk to Kevin calmly about noise at his townhouse without mentioning Jenna by name.
- Mention Ethan’s comments to Dr. Kline next Thursday.

metadata.title: Monday brain dump – work / money / Ethan

artifacts.content:
Event: Dr. Kline – therapy (lunch break)
Date: Thursday, 2025-02-13
Time: 12:10 p.m. – 12:55 p.m.
Location: Sunnybrook Health Sciences Centre – Women’s Mental Health Program, E Wing, 3rd floor

Notes:
- Every other Thursday slot with Dr. Hannah Kline.
- Bring journal pages from November 2023 (“Why I can’t go back after today”) about Kevin’s admission during the final mediation at the Family Mediation Services office on Yonge.
- Also want to talk about:
  • The October 2023 episode in T Wing ED (discharge summary: “Non-cardiac chest pain – likely anxiety”) and how it still makes me second-guess any chest twinge at my desk.
  • The MyChart thread “Follow-up re: panic episode after Ethan’s weekend away” – especially how Sundays feel when he’s coming back from Ajax.
  • Ethan telling me on a couple of Monday mornings, “Dad and Jenna were arguing again last night,” and whether I should document that anywhere or just focus on helping him regulate.
  • Anxiety about possible restructuring after Elena’s June 2024 email (“Upcoming restructuring – please keep confidential”) and what losing the hybrid schedule would mean for Ethan’s appointments and emergency pickups.

Logistics:
- Block calendar at work as “E Wing meeting” so no one tries to book over it.
- Pack a quick nut‑free sandwich and granola bar so I’m not starving in session.
- Text Mom if I’m running late for 3:15 pickup so Daniel can be on standby “family Uber” again.

Hi Sophie,

Long overdue catch‑up while Ethan is working on a Lego city on the living room floor.

First, can we skip anything expensive this summer? I hate even writing that, but money is tight. Since January 2024 I’ve been carrying about $7,500 on my Scotiabank Visa from the separation legal fees and all the out‑of‑pocket stuff for Ethan’s allergist and optometrist at the Toronto East Medical Centre. The file on my laptop is literally called “Post-Separation Finances 2024.xlsx” – very glamorous. A cottage weekend sounds amazing, but I can’t justify another big charge right now.

On top of that, work is in limbo. Back in June 2024, Elena sent a confidential note titled “Upcoming restructuring – please keep confidential” about our research and outpatient clinic department maybe merging with another unit in early 2025. If they reclassify my Senior Administrative Coordinator role and pull us onto the Bayview campus full‑time, I’ll lose the hybrid setup that’s saving my sanity. I keep thinking about how that would affect Ethan’s appointments, PD days, and my ability to dash out if there’s an asthma or allergy issue at school.

Emotionally, I’m… okay-ish. I’m still going to see Dr. Hannah Kline at the Women’s Mental Health Program in E Wing every other Thursday over lunch. She’s the one who helped me make sense of that October 2023 scare in T Wing ED (“Non-cardiac chest pain – likely anxiety” on the discharge summary) and the panic episode after one of Ethan’s weekends in Ajax – the one I messaged her about in MyChart.

And then there’s the fun co‑parenting layer. Ethan has mentioned a few times on Monday mornings that “Dad and Jenna were arguing again last night.” I’m trying hard not to go straight into lawyer-brain and start rereading the separation order from 311 Jarvis (Thompson-Separation-Order-Dec2023.pdf) for travel clauses and what‑ifs.

Anyway, tell me about Ottawa things instead. How’s work? Are you still doing trivia nights?

Love,
L

metadata.subject: Summer, money, and general life chaos

artifacts.content:
Event: “M – dinner Broadview”
Date: Saturday, 2025-03-08
Time: 6:30 p.m. – 10:30 p.m.
Location: Near Broadview Station (Mark’s neighborhood)

Notes:
- Alternate Saturday when Ethan is with Kevin in Ajax for the weekend per the Dec 2023 separation agreement (Ontario Court of Justice, 311 Jarvis – “Thompson-Separation-Order-Dec2023.pdf”). Double-check Kevin’s text about pickup time to make sure it doesn’t conflict with this.
- “M – dinner Broadview” = dinner with Mark Chen (graphic designer, lives near Broadview). Keep label vague in case Ethan ever glances at the calendar.
- Plan: meet at Broadview station, walk to that little Thai place Mark likes, maybe a drink after. Aim to be home by 10:30 so Sunday isn’t a write‑off.
- Budget check: remember there’s still ~$7,500 sitting on the Scotiabank Visa from legal fees + Toronto East Medical Centre bills (see “Post-Separation Finances 2024.xlsx”). Pick somewhere reasonable, no fancy tasting menus.
- Emotional check‑in: pay attention to how it feels balancing dating with everything else – Ethan’s Monday wobbles after Ajax, the anxiety stuff (October 2023 T Wing ED visit, MyChart thread “Follow-up re: panic episode after Ethan’s weekend away”), ongoing therapy with Dr. Kline in E Wing.

Logistics:
- Leave condo by 6:05 p.m. – 10 min walk to subway from Willowbank Crescent.
- Bring phone charger, Presto card, lip balm.
- Text Sophie a quick “made it there/back” so someone knows where I am.

Hi Claire,

Thanks again for agreeing to watch Ethan next Friday evening so I can go to that yoga thing.

I wanted to give you the full allergy/asthma rundown so you’re not guessing if anything comes up. When he was four, in May 2020, he had a really scary anaphylactic reaction after eating walnut banana bread at my parents’ place in Newmarket. We ended up calling 911 and the paramedics took us to Southlake Regional Health Centre, where he was monitored in the pediatric observation area for a few hours. I still have the discharge instructions in a folder labeled “Ethan – Allergy Emergency May 2020.” That’s why I’m so intense about checking ingredients.

Practically: he has a diagnosed tree nut allergy, especially to walnuts and pecans. I’ll leave his EpiPen Jr. on the kitchen counter with a note. Please don’t let him share food with anyone, and only offer him the snacks I leave out (they’re all safe/nut‑free). If you ever see hives, swelling around his lips or eyes, trouble breathing, or vomiting, just use the EpiPen in his outer thigh, call 911 immediately, then call me and his dad. I’ll write all the numbers on the fridge, but mine is (416) 555‑2398 and Kevin’s is (289) 555‑9034.

He also has mild, mostly exercise- and cold-induced asthma. There will be a blue Ventolin inhaler in his backpack; if he’s coughing a lot or says his chest feels tight, have him sit, take two puffs with the spacer, and rest. His last really bad flare was March 2022 after a wild indoor soccer game – no ER trips since then.

On the fun side: he loves drawing and Lego, and we usually keep Friday nights low-key – pasta, a movie, maybe some comic books. He wears glasses for nearsightedness and tends to misplace them when he gets tired, so a quick “glasses check” before bed helps.

Thank you again – I know it’s a lot of information, but once you’ve seen the routine it feels normal.

Laura

Hi Sophie,

I hope you're doing well over there in Ottawa. I wanted to update you on a few things regarding Ethan and our routine here in Toronto.

Firstly, the school recently asked for an update on Ethan's emergency contacts and transportation permissions. I've confirmed with them that Mom, Margaret Ellen Davis, remains Ethan's emergency contact; she's the one I trust most during the day. Her number is still (647) 555-7810. Dad, Daniel Robert Thompson, is on the list as well for pickups, given how much Ethan loves those drives with 'the family Uber'. His number is (905) 555-4462. I’ve made it clear that Ethan doesn't have permission to walk home alone yet, considering his age and health needs.

In other news, I've signed a media release form for Ethan's school activities. I gave permission for media releases because they often capture great moments in his school life, and I like having those memories. It's always heartwarming to see his photos in the school newsletter or on their website.

On a personal note, life is slowly finding a new rhythm post-separation. Ethan is adjusting well, though he still has those quiet Sundays after spending weekends with Kevin. I'm keeping our Sunday evenings calm and comforting, with pasta dinners and reading sessions.

Financially, things remain tight but manageable. Child support from Kevin, alongside my salary as a Senior Administrative Coordinator at Sunnybrook Health Sciences Centre, helps cover our needs, especially with Ethan's asthma and tree nut allergy costs. I've become quite adept at budgeting, even if it means stretching veggie chili over two nights!

I’m also trying to make more time for myself during the weekends when Ethan is with Kevin. Yoga classes and coffee with some old university friends have been a nice way to reconnect with myself.

Anyway, I hope this gives you a good picture of where we’re at. I miss having you nearby, but we’ll have to plan a visit soon. Give my love to everyone there!

Take care,
Laura

Laura M. Thompson
Date: 11/12/2025

metadata.subject: Updates on Ethan and Our New Routine
